what_it_does: Defines the core TypeScript interfaces and type aliases for recollection units: tool-call records, capture-source harness tags, conversation exchange metadata, and search-result wrappers.
observations: Declares ToolCall with id, exchangeId, toolName, optional toolInput/toolResult, isError, and timestamp.
Defines ConversationHarness as the union 'claude' | 'codex' | 'board' | 'clone' with a long comment explaining each source.

ConversationExchange carries project, timestamp, messages, archive location, lineage fields (parentUuid, isSidechain), session context (harness, sessionId, cwd, gitBranch, versions, model), thinking metadata, and an optional toolCalls array.
SearchResult pairs an exchange with a similarity score and a snippet.
MultiConceptResult pairs an exchange with a snippet and per-concept similarities plus an average.
Several fields are stored as serialized JSON strings (thinkingTriggers) or loose any (toolInput).
standouts: The ConversationHarness comment explicitly designs the source axis so that sources:['clone'] works through the same generic IN-filter as other harnesses.
MultiConceptResult exposes conceptSimilarities alongside an average, supporting concept-level retrieval diagnostics.
ConversationExchange mixes conversation lineage, session provenance, thinking metadata, and tool-call references in one interface.
concerns: toolInput is typed as any, weakening type safety.
toolResult is typed as string, which may not accommodate structured tool outputs.
thinkingTriggers is a string meant to hold a JSON array rather than a typed array.
No doc comments on individual fields beyond the harness enum comment.
notes: This file is the central type contract for the recollection module: it says what a captured exchange looks like and how search results wrap it.
questions: How are similarity and snippet computed for SearchResult and MultiConceptResult?
What is the complete set of allowed values for thinkingLevel?
How are parentUuid and isSidechain used to reconstruct conversation trees?
Where is the harness value normalized when an exchange is imported from board or clone sources?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of providing shared recollection types matches the file content.
intention_signals: The file tries to establish a single, generic schema that can represent conversation units captured from multiple harnesses or external sources, then feed them into a unified semantic search and multi-concept ranking pipeline.
novelty: The ConversationHarness source axis unifies conversation harnesses, noticeboard items, and clone-fleet reflections under one generic filterable field, and MultiConceptResult exposes per-concept similarity vectors rather than a single score.
contribution: Provides the shared data shapes that let capture sources, storage, and search results interoperate without special-casing each source; keeps the type surface small and focused on exchange provenance and retrieval wrappers.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript type definitions for tool-call records, capture-source harness tags, conversation exchange metadata, and semantic search result shapes in the recollection module.
intention_for_embedding: Establish a common, source-agnostic schema for storing and retrieving conversation recollections with multi-concept similarity search.

symbols.does: Describes a single tool invocation tied to an exchange.
symbols.description: ToolCall records the tool name, optional input and result, an error flag, and timestamps, linking the call back to an exchange via exchangeId.

symbols.does: Names the allowed capture-source tags for an exchange.
symbols.description: ConversationHarness is a union of 'claude', 'codex', 'board', and 'clone'; the accompanying comment explains that these values form a per-source axis used by a generic structural sources filter.

symbols.does: Defines the full shape of a stored conversation unit.
symbols.description: ConversationExchange combines message content, archive location, conversation lineage (parentUuid, isSidechain), session provenance (harness, sessionId, cwd, gitBranch, model), thinking metadata, and optional associated toolCalls.

symbols.does: Wraps an exchange with a single similarity score and snippet.
symbols.description: SearchResult is the basic retrieval output: it pairs a ConversationExchange with a numeric similarity score and a text snippet, presumably for ranking and display.

symbols.does: Wraps an exchange with per-concept similarities and an average.
symbols.description: MultiConceptResult extends the search-output idea by exposing a vector of conceptSimilarities and an averageSimilarity, enabling inspection of which concepts contributed to a match.
